segments.text: What advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit is there of circumcision?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Much every way: chiefly, because that unto them were committed the oracles of God.
For what if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: God forbid: yea, let God be true, but every man a liar;
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: as it is written,
That thou mightest be justified in thy sayings, and mightest overcome when thou art judged.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of God, what shall we say? Is God unrighteous who taketh vengeance?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: (I speak as a man)
God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For if the truth of God hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: And not rather,
(as we be slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say,)
Let us do evil, that good may come?
segments.speaker: Opponents

segments.text: whose damnation is just.
What then? are we better than they? No, in no wise: for we have before proved both Jews and Gentiles, that they are all under sin;
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: There is none righteous, no, not one:
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: They are all gone out of the way, they are together become unprofitable; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: Their throat is an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps is under their lips:
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: Whose mouth is full of cursing and bitterness:
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: Their feet are swift to shed blood:
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: Destruction and misery are in their ways:
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: And the way of peace have they not known:
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: There is no fear of God before their eyes.
segments.speaker: Scripture

segments.text: Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.
Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law is the knowledge of sin.
But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets;
Even the righteousness of God which is by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:
For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;
Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:
Whom God hath set forth to be a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;
To declare, I say, at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.
Where is boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.
Is he the God of the Jews only? is he not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also:
segments.speaker: Paul

segments.text: Seeing it is one God, which shall justify the circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.
Do we then make void the law through faith? God forbid: yea, we establish the law.
segments.speaker: Paul
